---
title: "FW 536 — Day 3 morning lab (v2 · terrestrial wildlife) — Your Name"
author: "Your Name Here"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document:
    self_contained: true
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 3
    toc_float: true
    code_folding: show
    theme: cosmo
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE)
```

# Day 3 morning lab — Hierarchical modeling problem set

**Problem set version:** **v2 — terrestrial wildlife theme**.
(The companion version is [v3 — aquatic & fisheries](problem_set_v3.html); you choose whichever theme fits your interests.)

This template matches [`problem_set_v2.html`](problem_set_v2.html). Read each problem statement there, write your work below, **Knit** to HTML, and upload the `.html` to the matching Canvas assignment.

For setup help see the [R Markdown tutorial](../rmarkdown_tutorial.html).

---

# Setup

```{r}
# Load packages you'll need. Add or remove as appropriate.
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(lme4)
library(nlme)
library(AICcmodavg)
library(MuMIn)
```

---

# Problem 1 — Cougar home-range size across mountain ranges

> A wildlife biologist measures the 95% MCP home-range size (km$^2$) of 60 GPS-collared cougars, distributed across 8 mountain ranges (5–10 cougars per range). The biological question: is there a real difference in mean home-range size among mountain ranges?

**My work:**

```{r}
# Your R code here
```

**Answer / biological interpretation:**


---

# Problem 2 — Spotted owl pair productivity across forests

> Over 6 years you monitor breeding success of 240 spotted owl pairs nested in 24 national forests. Each pair has its territory size (ha) measured. The response is fledglings produced. You fit:

**My work:**

```{r}
# Your R code here
```

**Answer / biological interpretation:**


---

# Problem 3 — Elk body mass vs age, across herds

> Body mass (kg) is measured on 480 elk across 12 herds. The biological question: how does body mass scale with age, and does the slope differ by herd?

**My work:**

```{r}
# Your R code here
```

**Answer / biological interpretation:**


---

# Problem 4 — Mule deer aerial counts nested in management units

> The Oregon Department of Fish & Wildlife flies aerial surveys: 3 flights × 6 transects per flight × 4 management units = 72 transect-flights, each producing a deer count. Counts vary among units, among transects within units, and among flights within transects.

**My work:**

```{r}
# Your R code here
```

**Answer / biological interpretation:**


---

# Problem 5 — Pacific marten detection probabilities across regions

> You fit a logistic mixed model to camera-trap data:

**My work:**

```{r}
# Your R code here
```

**Answer / biological interpretation:**


---

# (Optional) Embed a photo of hand-written work

For problems easier to do on paper, photograph and embed:

```{r, echo=FALSE, eval=FALSE, out.width="60%", fig.cap="Hand-derivation for Problem N"}
knitr::include_graphics("images/problemN_derivation.jpg")
```

(Change `eval=FALSE` to `eval=TRUE` after saving an image. See the [R Markdown tutorial](../rmarkdown_tutorial.html) for details.)

---

# Submission checklist

- [ ] Answered every morning problem from `problem_set_v2.html`.
- [ ] Knitted to HTML without errors.
- [ ] Photos (if any) embedded with `self_contained: true` in the YAML.
- [ ] Uploaded the `.html` to the matching Canvas assignment.
